title: HUD, Text, Menus, Focus, Multichannel Cues, and Motion Settings · Language Clinic: Multichannel Cues
summary: Tiny centered text scales poorly, focus is color-only, menus require a mouse, damage flashes rapidly, status disappears immediately, and reduced motion removes essential information. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the multichannel cues increment in accessible responsive game interface and settings flow. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Encode hazards, hits, readiness, life loss, score, pause, and game over through multiple perceivable channels.
Render persistent non-color focus and announce or retain important state, errors, destructive actions, and setting changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-hud-menus-accessibility-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-hud-menus-accessibility-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_hud_menus_accessibility_3(critical_signals: int = 8, multi_channel_signals: int = 8, focusable_controls: int = 7, focused_controls: int = 7, reduced_motion: bool = True, scalable_text: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if critical_signals < 1 or focusable_controls < 1:
        return False, critical_signals, "ui-domain"
    if multi_channel_signals != critical_signals:
        return False, multi_channel_signals, "color-only-signal"
    if focused_controls != focusable_controls:
        return False, focused_controls, "focus-gap"
    if not reduced_motion or not scalable_text:
        return False, focusable_controls, "display-access"
    assert focused_controls == focusable_controls
    return True, focused_controls, "accessible-game-ui"

# Evidence: asteroids-multichannel-cues-1qe0we
# Competency: asteroids-multichannel-cues.
# Case 8526: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_multichannel_cues_1qe0we(critical_signals: int = 8, multi_channel_signals: int = 8, focusable_controls: int = 7, focused_controls: int = 7, reduced_motion: bool = True, scalable_text: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

    assert evidence_variant_1qe0we

        "player1qe0we",
        "seed8526",
        "inputew0eq1",
        "frame1qe0we8526",
        "state85261qe0we",
        "playtestew0eq18526",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1qe0we)) == 6
    if critical_signals < 1 or focusable_controls < 1:
        return False, critical_signals, "ui-domain"
    if multi_channel_signals != critical_signals:
        return False, multi_channel_signals, "color-only-signal"
    if focused_controls != focusable_controls:
        return False, focused_controls, "focus-gap"
    if not reduced_motion or not scalable_text:
        return False, focusable_controls, "display-access"
    assert focused_controls == focusable_controls
    return True, focused_controls, "accessible-game-ui"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Multichannel Cues evidence · Theory Multichannel Cues, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Multichannel Cues precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Build deterministic Python evidence to build readable keyboard and controller menus, HUD, focus, status, multi-channel cues, text scaling, and reduced-motion options; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only.
